reasoning: Pragma's FIDE rank on May 1 (which defines the baseline for May 25) is #7 with 2758 Elo. In the May Superbet Romania tournament, he scored 5.5/9, which I calculated will net him around +9 Elo (to ~2767). Crucially, the player right behind him, Firouzja (2757), also scored 5.5/9 in the exact same round-robin field. Mathematically, since Pragg started +1 Elo higher, they both gain essentially the same amount (+9), maintaining Pragg's slim ~1 Elo lead over Firouzja (~2767 vs ~2766). Abdusattorov (2771) scored 4.5/9 and will lose a couple of points, likely dropping to ~2768, meaning Pragg might even leap to #6 if Abdusattorov loses slightly more, but will be safely #7 otherwise. With no major tournaments ending before June 1, Pragg is mathematically secured to be at least #7 (barring unknown unrated team matches for Firouzja). Therefore, the June dates have high YES probability as they will use the June 1 list. Over August and November, Pragg faces high variance because Firouzja, Wei Yi, and Nepomniachtchi will all be within ~10 Elo points waiting for him to stumble, pulling probabilities closer to base rates for rank-holding in a dense cluster.
